item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image could not be properly sourced and is assumed to be non-compliant. As such, it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet if Foundation keep unfolding this they'll found an incredible cross section page of SCP 2722.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The part about guiding the boy with the helmet to find his ship makes me fondly remember the game "commander keen" from way back. I used to play the shit out it, haha. Anyways, entertaining and well written so plus one! I would probably have up voted anyways due to the seeming reference to previously mentioned game.

Edit: Grats on the 100+!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: °* we are, ignore the typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe the universe IS on the paper, and were unravaling our own universe…..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe the universe is just puzzles all the way down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was thinking. It has a space theme and for as far as we know space could be infinite, like the piece of paper. What if it is actually a map of the entire universe? The universe is just so big that you would have to unfold the thing an impossible amount of times to see it all. I know it's puzzles and all but there could be a hidden meaning, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want this for reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me the super awesome perfect value could be decreased by hinting that the puzzles become progressively more disturbing/Colour Out Of Space stuff (or at least 'how the hell did they get this information?') the further you unfold it; there seems to be some suggestion of that in the article as it stands with the references to the huge encrypted communication and mysterious Mars Lander schematics. Of course, this is intended to be a Safe SCP, so ymmv :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can see where you're coming from, but I don't really see this as a super awesome toy. After a half-dozen unfoldings it becomes increasingly unwieldy and the puzzles stop being fun for kids and instead unsolvable or unmanageable by not just kids but anyone. It's only fun up to a point.

An awesome infinity puzzle book would be one which had a traditional page-turning structure, but there was no last page, it just went on and on and all the puzzles were fun and solvable.

Still, not everything clicks for everyone. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given that Bright and I are the only ones to downvote, I feel like I should give some sort of explanation.

I honestly don't like the idea of Wondertainment items that are basically "super awesome perfect kids toy, and maybe there's some downside". I can personally only envision Wondertainment as "You humans don't find this whimsical?". If we actually find something cool and amusing, then it doesn't work as Wondertainment for me.

Further, if Wondertainment was removed from this, it'd just be an infinite puzzle book. Something out-of-reach of anything a kid could do, certainly, but it's still just a fun puzzlebook. It doesn't grab me at all, or evoke any sort of emotion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you unwrap it enough, you'll find a golden ticket.

I love this. Wondertainment is my favorite. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda pictured him like that, but not so much malevolent so much as batcrap bonkers with a dash of Cave Johnson.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting question! Considering that if you write on it the writing is still there when it's re-opened, I'm going to say yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Folding it reduces the weight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I never thought of Dr. Wondertainment being an actual person, just a brand. But I like the idea of him (it?) being a Willy Wonka like character, but even more malevolent and mysterious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Question: if something is placed inside the book as it is folded back up, will it still be there when it is unfolded again?

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wondering that too.

Anyway, other than that little plot hole, I love this. I kind of envision the Bring Your Kids to Work Day at the Foundation being filled with bored kids doing this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent Wondertainment item, especially as it's not going to kill anyone. Most likely.

My question is: when you refold it, does the weight then decrease at the same rate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember when this was mentioned. I liked it then and I like it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point. Maybe I'll bump it up to 5m x 5m….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's fairly odd indeed, though not impossible, I think, if you consider that it is folded. Mine was folded inside a 20 cm x 15 cm comic book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! It's hard to match up to some of the best Dr. Wondertainment SCPs (Robodude is my personal favorite), but I'm glad I captured the spirit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, those are definitely real. I could've made it bigger, but I was building up to the weirder stuff. A 2m x 2m crossword in a 12cm x 20cm book is mighty odd, I hope!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted! It's a lovely "little" SCP and I like the idea a lot. Really not bad, I think you got the Dr. Wondertainment mood just right (I know I'm a newbie, but I love Wondertainment stuff).
Also commenting to point out that I once owned a crossword puzzle that was almost 2 meters long (it was around 1,50x1,50). I never got to complete it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You'll never run out of fun! Guaranteed!*

*Guarantee is not legally binding.

Upvoted as soon as I read this.

Beyond the central gimmick of Dr. Wondertainment being Willy Wonka + 343, I love it when legal disclaimers and such are added to DW items. It makes it seem more real somehow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it remains a .5cm wodge, is this necessary? Surely there would be at most a couple of visible folds each time.

Some folds and some page corners, but yet it's impossible to count how many there are at any one time. I preferred that over having to specify how the number of pages would have to change during an unfolding.

I note the crossword in the image does not share the space exploration theme, though I think that's a nit-pick :) Had a quick Google to see if I could find any photos of a 'world's largest crossword' with space references and came up empty, so please feel free to disregard.

Yeah. It was the best image I could find so I had to make some allowances for non-space puzzles. Puzzles at higher numbers can just be simple puzzles, but the general trend is for more complex and weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea and have upvoted - do you envisage the 'puzzles' getting increasingly arcane and Forbidden Knowledge-y as it's extended? Seems that way with the progress you've shown from samples 3 to 1038, but 1037 could easily be something more innocuous (update: just noticed you've stated the puzzles increase in 'size, complexity and difficulty', though perhaps a mention of the information involved becoming more sensitive wouldn't go amiss). The only bit I had difficulty visualising was this:

"No attempt to count the number of remaining pages has succeeded; humans invariably lose count and machines produce a variety of errors."

If it remains a .5cm wodge, is this necessary? Surely there would be at most a couple of visible folds each time.

I note the crossword in the image does not share the space exploration theme, though I think that's a nit-pick :) Had a quick Google to see if I could find any photos of a 'world's largest crossword' with space references and came up empty, so please feel free to disregard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a sucker for anything Wondertainment, and this is no exception. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first point you make is a good one. I'll add in something to that effect.

As to the latter point, it's not just one sheet you're unfolding. It's a half-centimeter stack of paper no matter how many times you unfold it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I could think about when you made your secound point was the Mythbusters trying to fold a peice of paper more than 7 times in an airplane hanger. Also good SCP +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems like an excellent idea, and well written. I had a couple of question points as I read. After describing the unfolding, I immediately wanted to know if it could be refolded. You imply that it can be during the description, as some activities are done inside but the paper was found in original dimension condition, but don't say so directly until the addendum.
Also, I'm not sure the weight would really be the issue in unfolding to that size - even a sheet at the largest size you indicate probably wouldn't require 50 people to flip, I shouldn't think. It seems the unwieldiness of flipping something that big and floppy would be the worst part.

Two totally quibbling points. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would you trust something built from a Dr. Wondertainment schematic? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DAMN YOUUUUUUUUUUUU!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DO NOT QUESTION ROGET.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did they try to build the Mars lander? Please tell me they tried to build the Mars lander!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, way better than the hundred pound fish tank (HOW IS THAT FOR KIDS?!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. :) I had the idea for an infinitely unfolding piece of paper, and was trying to think about something creepy and mysterious to have as text. Then I realized it'd make a perfect Wondertainment device.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh.

Well, that aside, this is cute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was recently informed that I should either tag the SCP completely, meaning identify all potential attributes correctly, or leave it blank for a mod script to find. I wasn't certain I could correctly tag this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where are your tags!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP and my first Dr. Wondertainment SCP. Open to crits, comments, and suggestions.
